results.title: Reuse your FLOPs: Scaling RL on Hard Problems by Conditioning on Very Off-Policy Prefixes (arXiv 2026)
results.snippet: Amrith Setlur et al. 2026. Reuse your FLOPs: scaling RL on hard problems. Addresses how RL methods for LLM reasoning waste compute on hard problems where correct on-policy traces are rare.

results.title: RL on Incorrect Synthetic Data Scales the Efficiency of LLM Math Reasoning by Eight-Fold
results.snippet: Amrith Setlur et al. NeurIPS 2024. Using incorrect synthetic data, hard math problems become tractable; RL scales 8x in efficiency.

results.title: [2407.04620] Learning to (Learn at Test Time): RNNs with Expressive Hidden States
results.snippet: Yu Sun et al. ICML 2025. Hard problems in long-context language modeling tackled via test-time training on data.

Yu Sun.query: Yu Sun model test time 2024 OR 2025 OR 2026

results.title: [2512.23675] End-to-End Test-Time Training for Long Context
results.snippet: End-to-End Test-Time Training for Long Context. Authored by Arnuv Tandon, Karan Dalal, ..., Yu Sun. Long-context language modeling as a problem in continual learning rather than architecture design; the model continues learning at test time via next-token prediction on the given context. For 3B models trained with 164B tokens, TTT-E2E scales with context length like full-attention Transformer; Mamba 2 and Gated DeltaNet do not.

results.title: [2407.04620] Learning to (Learn at Test Time): RNNs with Expressive Hidden States
results.snippet: Yu Sun et al. Learning at test time: RNNs with hidden states that update on context. Test-Time Training (TTT) layers as innovative RNN modules with adaptive neural network states that dynamically adjust during inference. ICML 2025.

results.title: Test-Time Training Project Website
results.snippet: Yu Sun's Test-Time Training research project page. Train a different model on-the-fly for each test instance — TTT. Long context modeling, language models, Mamba alternative.

results.title: Yu Sun | Stanford Trustworthy AI Research
results.snippet: Yu Sun's current research direction is training a different model on-the-fly for each test instance, which he calls test-time training. Test-time training papers in 2024 and 2025.
